evals.expected_output: Skill detects git repo, analyzes staged changes, notices README is unrelated to main feature change, asks user whether to include it, then generates a Conventional Commits message with Chinese description and proposes it before running git commit.
evals.setup: git repo with staged src/auth.js (new JWT feature) and staged README.md (unrelated changelog entry)

assertions.description: Asks user whether to include README.md before committing, because it is unrelated to the main feature
Proposes commit type `feat` (new functionality being added)
Uses scope `auth` matching the changed module
Commit description is written in Chinese
Shows proposed message and waits for user confirmation before running git commit

evals.expected_output: Skill detects jj repo, analyzes diff showing a bug fix in auth module, generates fix(auth): <Chinese description>, confirms with user, then runs jj describe -m ...
evals.setup: jj (colocated) repo with changes to src/auth/session.ts fixing null reference errors

assertions.description: Detects .jj/ directory and uses jj commands (jj status, jj diff) instead of git
Proposes commit type `fix` (bug fix, not a new feature)
Uses scope `auth` matching the changed module
Commit description is written in Chinese
Uses `jj describe -m` (not `git commit` or `jj commit`) after confirmation
Shows proposed message and waits for user confirmation

evals.expected_output: Skill detects git repo, finds no staged files but unstaged changes exist, informs user and asks which files to include (does NOT silently stage everything), waits for guidance.
evals.setup: git repo with unstaged changes in app.py (new feature + refactor) and config.py (config tweak), nothing in staging area

assertions.description: Does NOT silently run git add -A or stage files without asking
Informs user that nothing is staged and shows what unstaged files exist
Notices or mentions that app.py and config.py have different concerns (feature vs config change)
Asks the user which files to include rather than making the decision unilaterally
Does not attempt to run git commit before the user decides what to stage
